sourceMovie.title: Blood Diamond
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Blood Diamond (2006) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Edward Zwick's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Captain Phillips
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blood Diamond clicked for you, Captain Phillips (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blood Diamond clicked for you, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blood Diamond?
faq.answer: Try Captain Phillips, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, Repo Men—each shares DNA with Blood Diamond while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blood Diamond?
faq.answer: Blood Diamond (2006) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blood Diamond worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
